metadata.description: Real in-situ water quality data for Lake Titicaca extracted from open-source government and peer-reviewed publications. Used as validation context for Harmonized Sentinel-2/Landsat ensemble ML model.
metadata.authors: Fred Torres-Cruz, Dina Maribel Yana-Yucra, Richar Andre Vilca-Solorzano
metadata.purpose: Validation context and trophic reference for Chl-a and turbidity monitoring in Lake Titicaca (Peruvian sector)
metadata.parameters_note: Turbidity in NTU not directly reported in OEFA/IMARPE field reports (Secchi disk and SST mg/L used instead). Direct NTU in-situ data from ANA/PEBLT campaigns reported in Maligaya et al. 2024 (range 0-7 NTU, mostly <3 NTU for Lago Mayor/Menor).
metadata.coordinate_system_note: OEFA stations converted from UTM Zone 19S to WGS84 decimal degrees (approximate, +/-0.001 deg)

sources.citation: OEFA (2016). Informe N° 076-2016-OEFA/DE-SDCA: Evaluacion de la calidad del agua de la Bahia Interior de Puno - Lago Titicaca. Organismo de Evaluacion y Fiscalizacion Ambiental, Lima, Peru.
sources.location: Bahia Interior Puno (near PTAR El Espinar wastewater treatment plant)
sources.notes: Chl-a reported in mg/L (equivalent to g/m3). Values 0.202-0.413 mg/L = 202-413 ug/L, consistent with hypertrophic classification (OECD: >25 ug/L = eutrophic; >75 ug/L = hypertrophic). SST = Total Suspended Solids (mg/L), not NTU turbidity.

sources.citation: Siguayro, R.A. and Franco, E. (2022). Evaluacion limnologica del Lago Titicaca - Sector Peruano (Julio 2019). Informe Instituto del Mar del Peru, 49(4), 538-551.
sources.notes: July = austral winter dry season. Chl-a values substantially lower than wet season due to seasonal dynamics. Transparency measured as Secchi disk depth (m). No NTU turbidity reported.

sources.citation: Siguayro, R.A., Pasapera, J., Villanueva, C., Coila, P. and Gamarra, S. (2022). Evaluacion de fuentes contaminantes en el anillo circumlacustre del Lago Titicaca. Boletin Instituto del Mar del Peru, 37(2), 361-386. doi:10.53554/boletin.v37i2.378
sources.coverage: Circumlacustrine ring - rivers, wastewater effluents, bays/river mouths
sources.notes: Trophic classification based on OECD criteria. Chl-a in mg/m3 (= ug/L).

bay_surface_chl_a.note: Low mean because non-bloom period; hypertrophic confirmed by max values

sources.citation: Maligaya, V.H.M., Baltodano, A., Agramont, A. and van Griensven, A. (2024). Exploring Trends and Variability of Water Quality over Lake Titicaca Using Global Remote Sensing Products. Remote Sensing, 16, 4785. doi:10.3390/rs16244785
sources.insitu_data_source: ANA (Autoridad Nacional del Agua, Peru) via PEBLT (Proyecto Especial Binacional Lago Titicaca)
sources.insitu_data_note: Dataset available on request from corresponding author (van Griensven)

CGLS_turbidity_vs_insitu.note: Very low R2 - global product poorly calibrated for high-altitude lakes

ESA_CCI_turbidity_vs_insitu.note: Very low R2 - global product poorly calibrated for high-altitude lakes

water_quality_hotspots_appendixC.note: 13 WQH locations identified for spatiotemporal variability analysis

insitu_turbidity_range_from_figures.note: Extracted visually from Figure A2 scatter plots (RS vs in-situ turbidity)

trophic_context_summary.description: Trophic state reference values for Lake Titicaca zones, consistent across all sources

Bahia_Puno.note: Strong seasonal signal; wet season (Nov-Mar) bloom dominates annual mean

citation_for_manuscript.note: Use these citations when referencing real in-situ data in the manuscript
latex_refs: \bibitem{OEFA2016} OEFA, ``Informe N° 076-2016-OEFA/DE-SDCA: Evaluacion de la calidad del agua de la Bahia Interior de Puno -- Lago Titicaca,'' Organismo de Evaluacion y Fiscalizacion Ambiental, Lima, Peru, 2016.

\bibitem{Siguayro2022a} R.A. Siguayro and E. Franco, ``Evaluacion limnologica del Lago Titicaca -- Sector Peruano (Julio 2019),'' \textit{Informe Instituto del Mar del Peru}, vol. 49, no. 4, pp. 538--551, 2022.

\bibitem{Siguayro2022b} R.A. Siguayro, J. Pasapera, C. Villanueva, P. Coila and S. Gamarra, ``Evaluacion de fuentes contaminantes en el anillo circumlacustre del Lago Titicaca,'' \textit{Boletin Instituto del Mar del Peru}, vol. 37, no. 2, pp. 361--386, 2022, doi: 10.53554/boletin.v37i2.378.

\bibitem{Maligaya2024} V.H.M. Maligaya, A. Baltodano, A. Agramont and A. van Griensven, ``Exploring Trends and Variability of Water Quality over Lake Titicaca Using Global Remote Sensing Products,'' \textit{Remote Sensing}, vol. 16, p. 4785, 2024, doi: 10.3390/rs16244785.
